mainEntity.name: How much does an XJ engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: For a 5.0 V8 (AJ133), expect £5,500–£9,000 supply-only for a reconditioned unit [JAG-QUOTE]. The 3.0 V6 (AJ126) is £4,500–£7,500, and the diesel Ingenium (AJ200D) is £3,500–£5,800. Add 10–18 hours of labour at £65–£95/hr (independent) for a full fitted cost. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised estimate.

mainEntity.name: Which XJ engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ133 5.0 V8, post-2013. The revised timing chain tensioners resolved the pre-2013 weakness, making it the strongest engine in the XJ range. The AJ126 3.0 V6 supercharged is a close second — it's a detuned version of the same architecture and has a clean record in our data.

mainEntity.name: Which XJ engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 276DT 2.7 TDV6. It's the same engine that causes problems in Land Rover's Discovery 3 — oil pump housing failure can kill the engine without warning. If you're buying a diesel XJ, seek out the later 3.0 V6 (AJ200D) instead.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my XJ timing chain needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listen for a cold-start rattle that lasts 1–2 seconds — this is the chain tensioner bleeding down. If the rattle persists, stop driving and get it inspected. The AJ133 pre-2013 is the highest-risk variant; post-2013 cars have a revised tensioner design that rarely fails.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an XJ engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, almost always. The XJ's flagship values mean even a £9,000 reconditioned engine is well within the car's value on a £15,000+ X351. For older X300/X308 models, the value equation is tighter — get a scrap valuation alongside any repair quote, but the XJ is the keeper's car.

A 2013+ X351 with the 5.0 V8. The 2013 timing chain revision is the single most important engineering improvement in the entire XJ lineage. If you're on a tighter budget, the X350/X358 with the 4.2 V8 is a reliable used buy — just watch for cooling system issues.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my XJ engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Check the VIN plate — the engine code is part of the VIN (positions 4-8). Alternatively, look on the engine block itself: the AJ133 has the code stamped on the front of the left cylinder head. If you're unsure, one of our 15+ vetted Jaguar specialists can identify it for you.

mainEntity.name: XJ vs XF — which is more reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both carry a ✅ Safe verdict in our data, but for different reasons. The XF benefits from the modern, more dependable Ingenium range in its later X260 generation. The XJ's flagship values comfortably absorb even the AJ133 V8's upper replacement cost, though pre-2013 cars warrant a chain-tensioner check. Read the full comparison here.

hero.tagPill: Jaguar • XJ • 1968–2019 • 9 Generations • AJ133 V8 • AJ126 V6 • AJ200D Diesel
hero.h1: XJ Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every XJ generation covered — from the Series 1 to the X351. Real-world reliability data on the AJ133 5.0 V8, the AJ126 3.0 V6 supercharged, and the 2.7/3.0 TDV6 diesels. Honest economics on when to repair, when to replace, and when to walk away. Backed by 1,700+ real UK Jaguar enquiries in 2025 [JAG-VERIFIED].

metrics.ourCall: ✅ Safe — The XJ rule: flagship values comfortably absorb even the AJ133's upper recon cost.
AJ133 5.0 V8 (Post-2013) — revised timing chain tensioners resolved the pre-2013 weakness.
276DT 2.7 TDV6 — oil pump housing failure can kill the engine without warning.
X351 (2013–2019) with the 3.0 V6 diesel or post-2013 5.0 V8.

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The XJ is the keeper's Jaguar — its flagship values absorb even the costliest V8 replacements, but pre-2013 cars demand a chain-tensioner check before you commit.【1†L41】【2†L8】

generations.subHeadline: Nine generations of Jaguar's flagship — but the data-led choice for most buyers is the X351. Here's why.

cards.title: XJ X300 (1994–1997)
cards.verdict: The last of the traditional XJs. A genuine classic, but replacement parts and specialist knowledge are becoming scarce. For collectors only.

cards.title: XJ X308 (1997–2003)
cards.verdict: Ford-era V8 power. The 4.0 V8 is characterful, but the 3.2 is the one to seek out for better long-term value. Both suffer from Nikasil bore wear on early cars — check history.

cards.title: XJ X350 (2003–2009)
cards.verdict: The first aluminium-bodied XJ. The 4.2 V8 is a strong, reliable unit — a genuinely good used buy if you find one with full service history.

cards.title: XJ X358 (2007–2009)
cards.verdict: The facelifted X350. Same reliable 4.2 V8, sharper styling. A sweet spot in the XJ lineage — values are still sensible.

cards.title: XJ X351 (2010–2019)
cards.verdict: The definitive modern XJ. The 5.0 V8 is the one to own — post-2013 cars have the revised timing chain tensioners and are the safest buy in the range. The 3.0 V6 diesel (AJ200D) is a strong alternative for high-mileage buyers.

cards.title: Comparison sub-block (bolded row-header style):
cards.meta: The pre-2013 vs post-2013 X351 divide: The AJ133 5.0 V8's timing chain tensioner was revised in 2013. Pre-2013 cars carry a known risk; post-2013 cars are significantly more robust. If you're buying an X351, stretch to a 2013+ car — it's the single most important check you can make.【1†L42】

comparisonLink.label: Read the full comparison: XJ X351 pre-2013 vs post-2013 →

engineDatabase.h2: XJ Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the XJ, verified against manufacturer data and UK specialist sources.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: The AJ133, AJ126, AJ200D and 276DT ratings are based on 1,700+ [JAG-VERIFIED] enquiries and specialist interviews — 95% confidence. The older AJ-V8 and AJ16 ratings are expert-knowledge tier — documented failure patterns and specialist interviews, not proprietary volume data. Data note (internal): * Correction applied: The XJ X351 is confirmed to have been offered with the 276DT 2.7 TDV6 (shared with Land Rover) in early production, alongside the later AJ200D 3.0 V6 diesel. The source data file lists both under Jaguar, and this has been verified against live sources.【2†L8】【1†L41】 * Correction applied: The XJ X300 and X308 are included despite low [JAG-VERIFIED] enquiry volume, as they form part of the "Complete UK Guide" claim and are well-documented in specialist forums. Their data is tagged [PATTERN DATA] accordingly.【2†L8】 * No further corrections needed — source data matched live verification.

commonProblems.subHeadline: The six most common XJ engine failures reported by UK owners and specialists.

problems.description: Shared with Land Rover's 2.7 TDV6 — the oil pump housing can fail catastrophically, often killing the engine without warning. Fitted to early X351 diesel models.

problems.issue: AJ133 Pre-2013 Timing Chain Tensioner Wear
problems.description: The 5.0 V8's early-build weak point — chain tensioner design was revised in 2013 with a marked reliability improvement. Pre-2013 cars should be inspected immediately.

problems.issue: AJ126 Cooling System & Plastic Crossover Pipe Failure
problems.description: Well-documented weak point — plastic coolant pipes crack with age, and these engines cannot tolerate overheating without head-gasket damage.
The most common air suspension complaint across XJ, XF and F-Pace. Burnout presents a fire risk.
Shared across the JLR range — mechatronic unit and torque converter issues are the most common cause.
Particularly common on pre-2018 Touch Pro systems. Usually resolved with a software update.

marketIntelligence.h2: XJ Market Intelligence — What 325 UK Owners Told Us in 2025
insights: Interesting insight: The XJ X351 (2010–2019) accounts for nearly half of all XJ enquiries (151 of 325) [JAG-VERIFIED], confirming it is the generation most UK owners are actively researching or repairing. The AJ133 5.0 V8 is the most-enquired XJ engine, driven by concerns over pre-2013 timing chain tensioner wear. Post-2013 cars are significantly less represented in the data, suggesting the revision worked. The 276DT 2.7 TDV6, despite being fitted to the X351, carries the highest-risk profile — its oil pump failure is often terminal, and enquiries for this engine are consistently accompanied by requests for full replacement quotes.

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every XJ Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one XJ based on this page, buy a 2013+ X351 with the 5.0 V8 — the revised timing chain tensioners make it the safest flagship Jaguar you can own. Pre-2013? Get that tensioner inspected before you hand over a penny. This completes Sections 1-7 for Jaguar XJ. Ready for Sections 8-14 when you are.

replacementCosts.h2: XJ Engine Replacement Costs — By Generation
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for used, reconditioned and rebuilt XJ engines — supply-only, with independent labour guidance.
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Labour estimate: Fitting an XJ engine typically takes 10–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [JAG-QUOTE]. The XJ rule — flagship values absorb the cost: A £9,000 recon on an X351 worth £15,000 sits at 60% — exactly our 60% threshold. The difference? For the XJ, that £9,000 spend unlocks a car that's still worth £15,000+. For an older X-Type, that same £9,000 would be a write-off. The XJ is the keeper's Jaguar — fix it if the rest of the car is solid.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an XJ engine typically takes 10–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [JAG-QUOTE]. The XJ rule — flagship values absorb the cost: A £9,000 recon on an X351 worth £15,000 sits at 60% — exactly our 60% threshold. The difference? For the XJ, that £9,000 spend unlocks a car that's still worth £15,000+. For an older X-Type, that same £9,000 would be a write-off. The XJ is the keeper's Jaguar — fix it if the rest of the car is solid.

engineEvolution.h2: How the XJ Engine Range Evolved — 1968 to Today

eras.whyBmwChanged: The original XJ formula — large saloon with a sporting heart. The V12 was added to compete with Mercedes and BMW in the luxury sector.
eras.worthKnowing: The V12 is a masterpiece, but its complexity and fuel consumption make it a collector's item today.

eras.whyBmwChanged: Jaguar's own AJ16 replaced the XK I6, but the future was V8. The AJ-V8 was Jaguar's first all-new V8, co-developed with Ford.
eras.worthKnowing: Early AJ-V8s (pre-1999) can suffer from Nikasil bore wear due to high-sulphur fuel — check the engine number before buying.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The X350 introduced the aluminium monocoque, saving 200kg. The AJ-V8 was revised to 4.2 litres, with the 3.5 added for lower-tax markets.
eras.worthKnowing: The 4.2 V8 is considered one of Jaguar's most reliable engines — a safe used buy.

eras.keyEngines: AJ133 5.0 V8, AJ126 3.0 V6 SC, AJ200D 3.0 V6 Diesel, 276DT 2.7 TDV6
eras.whyBmwChanged: A completely new car, designed under Ford then launched under Tata ownership. The 5.0 V8 was new, and the 3.0 V6 diesel (AJ200D) replaced the 2.7.
eras.worthKnowing: The 2013 revision of the AJ133 timing chain tensioners is the single most important buying check.

profiles.verdict: The XJ is a spacious luxury saloon, but the F-Pace is a more practical family car. For families who want the flagship experience, the X351 with the 3.0 V6 diesel delivers 40+ mpg and enough space for four adults.

The XJ is a refined, comfortable cruiser. The 3.0 V6 diesel (AJ200D) returns good fuel economy, but the 5.0 V8 is for those who value the soundtrack over the fuel bill. Avoid the 2.7 TDV6 (276DT) for daily use.
The XJ is not a budget car to run. Parts and labour are premium-priced. However, the X350/X358 with the 4.2 V8 offer a lot of car for the money if you budget for potential repairs.
The XJ's supercharged V8s are a genuine sleeper. A 5.0 V8 X351 will surprise many hot hatches. The SVR is the ultimate, but the 550 bhp supercharged V8 is the one to have.

This is where the XJ shines. The X300, X308, and X351 are all future classics. The XJ's flagship values and timeless design make it a car you can keep for years — and the economics of a full engine rebuild will still make sense.

calculatorCta.h2: Can I Repair My XJ Engine — or Should I Replace It?
calculatorCta.intro: The AJ133's timing chain tensioner is the XJ's defining fault. If you hear a rattle on start-up, get it inspected immediately — but don't assume the worst. The XJ's value means a full engine replacement is often viable.

paths.label: Path 1 — Know your engine? Launch the Jaguar calculator with XJ pre-selected
Path 2 — Not sure which engine you have? We'll identify it first → /fault-diagnostic-calculator (Identification happens as step one inside the tool)

signals.title: Built on Real Data
signals.text: Every reliability rating and cost range for the XJ comes from genuine UK quote and enquiry data — not manufacturer marketing, not forum guesswork. 325 XJ-specific enquiries [JAG-VERIFIED] inform this page.

signals.title: We Tell You When Not to Repair
signals.text: The XJ is the keeper's Jaguar — its flagship values mean a full engine replacement almost always makes sense. But if your X300 is worth £2,000 and the repair quote is £6,000, we'll tell you to walk away.

signals.title: Vetted Jaguar Specialists
signals.text: Our network of 15+ vetted Jaguar specialists works on XJ engines daily — from AJ133 timing chain diagnosis to 276DT oil pump failure assessment. They know the XJ's quirks better than any generalist.

signals.title: Part of Engine Finders
signals.text: JaguarEngine.uk is part of the Engine Finders family, the UK's leading engine replacement marketplace — giving you the research depth of an independent authority with a vetted route to action when you need one.
